{{< include macros.qmd >}}

## Magnetic Resonance Elastography {.scrollable}


Aim: Remote palpation by shear waves, i.e. tomographic quantification of shear modulus of biological tissue.




::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}

- Induce excitation at a given frequency (e.g. 50Hz).


- Measure (complex) displacements $\blue{\bfu(\bfx)}$ via MRI (Motion encoding gradient).



- Invert displacements to find 
shear modulus $\red{G(\bfx)}$: viscoelastic properties

:::
::: {.column width="40%"}


![](Figs_for_MRE/Set1.png){width=95%}





:::

:::


A: T1/T2 weighted} B: $\vert \bfu(\bfx)\vert$ C: $\vert G(\bfx)\vert\qquad$. 

![](Figs_for_MRE/Set2.png){width=100%}

Images From: Tan CH, Venkatesh SK. MRE and Other Magnetic Resonance Imaging Techniques in Chronic Liver Disease: Current Status and Future Directions.  Gut and Liver 2016;10:672-686



## Forward/Inverse Problem in MRE {.scrollable}

::: callout-note

## Forward Model

Time-harmonic isotropic linear viscoelastic equations
$$
\rho \omega^2 \blue{\bfu} +\nabla \cdot \big[ \red{G} (\nabla \blue{\bfu} +(\nabla \blue{\bfu})^{T})\big]+\nabla (\red{\lambda} \nabla \cdot \blue{\bfu})=\mathbf{0},\qquad \text{in}~ \Omega,
$$
:::

- Known quantities:  $\omega$ (frequency) and $\rho\approx 10^{3}\text{kg}/\text{m}^3$.  \\

- Unknown quantities: BCs, $\lambda(\bfx)$ and $G(\bfx)$

- Measured quantities (via MRI):  $\bfu_{M}\equiv (\bfu(\bfx_{1}),\dots,\bfu(\bfx_{M}))$



::: callout-important

## Inverse Problem

Find the shear modulus $G(\bfx)=G_{s}(\bfx)+iG_{l}(\bfx),$ given measurements of  $\bfu_{M}\equiv (\bfu(\bfx_{1}),\dots,\bfu(\bfx_{M}))$.

:::

$\red{G_{s}(\bfx)}$: storage modulus. $\red{G_{l}(\bfx)}$: loss modulus. 


## Characterisation: 



![](Figs_for_MRE/for_talk1.png){width=100%}


::: backhub
↩ <a href="index.html">Volver al inicio</a>
:::
